task: Document the architectural decision faithfully for the Lua VM compiler repair. Decision: choose Approach B, Typed AST / Parse IR then lowering, as the only correct architecture for a real VM. Capture why recursive emit/direct-to-bytecode parser is invalid in Lalin, why emit is CFG splicing not calls, why parser must produce typed products, why lowering must own register allocation/patching/constants/upvalues/protos, and how this follows explicit_programming.md and LANGUAGE_REFERENCE.md. No new analysis; compile the prior workflow context and user decision into a self-sustaining markdown artifact.
lastOutputPreview:
# Architectural Decision: Lua VM Compiler Repair

## Goal

Enable the Lua VM source compiler in `experiments/lua_interpreter_vm` to compile real Lua source without recursive Lalin `emit` expansion, by replacing the current recursive-descent/direct-to-bytecode parser with a typed parse product layer and a separate lowering phase that owns bytecode generation.

## Incentives

The current compiler architecture treats Lalin regions as if they were runtime parser calls. That is invalid in Lalinl
